leadText: The X Accuracy (Japanese: ヨクアタール Hits Well) is a type of battle item introduced in Generation I. It temporarily boosts a Pokémon's accuracy.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generations I to II 1.2 Generations III to VI 1.3 Generation VII onward Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generations I to II [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag during battle, it makes the Pokémon's moves ignore accuracy checks. It also allows one-hit knockout moves to always hit but will still fail if the target has a higher Speed (in Generation I) or level (in Generation II) than the user. Generations III to VI [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag during battle, it raises the accuracy of a Pokémon currently in battle by 1 level. It also increases the Pokémon's friendship a little. One-hit knockout moves are no longer affected by X Accuracy. Generation VII onward [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag during battle, it raises the accuracy of a Pokémon currently in battle by 2 levels. It also increases the Pokémon's friendship a little. One-hit knockout moves are unaffected by X Accuracy.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad Temporarily heightens accuracy for one battle. G S C Raises accuracy. (1 BTL) R S E Colo XD Raises the accuracy of attack moves during one battle. FR LG Raises the accuracy stat of Pok é mon in battle. Wears off if the Pok é mon is withdrawn. D P Pt HG SS B W B2 W2 An item that raises the accuracy of a Pokémon in battle. It wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn. X Y OR AS An item that boosts the accuracy of a Pokémon during a battle. It wears off once the Pokémon is withdrawn. S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA An item that sharply boosts the accuracy of a Pokémon during a battle. It wears off once the Pokémon is withdrawn. S V An item that sharply boosts a Pokémon’s accuracy in battle. This effect disappears if the Pokémon leaves the battle.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Masters X Accuracy: Raises the user's accuracy by two stat ranks. X Accuracy All: Raises the accuracy of all allied sync pairs by two stat ranks.

sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 Price 1.2 Effect 1.2.1 Generations I to II 1.2.2 Generations III to VI 1.2.3 Generation VII onward 1.3 Wonder Launcher 1.4 Description 1.5 Acquisition In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] Price [ edit | edit source ] Games Cost Sell price R G B Y / R B Y G S C R S E FR LG D P Pt HG SS B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS P E BD SP $ 950 $ 475 S M US UM Sw Sh $ 1,000 $ 500 S V $ 1,000 $ 250 At Join Avenue 's Market, X Accuracy can be bought in dozens at $ 8550 each, which equates to $ 712.5 per X Accuracy. Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generations I to II [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag during battle, it makes the Pokémon's moves ignore accuracy checks. It also allows one-hit knockout moves to always hit but will still fail if the target has a higher Speed (in Generation I) or level (in Generation II) than the user. Generations III to VI [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag during battle, it raises the accuracy of a Pokémon currently in battle by 1 level. It also increases the Pokémon's friendship a little. One-hit knockout moves are no longer affected by X Accuracy. Generation VII onward [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag during battle, it raises the accuracy of a Pokémon currently in battle by 2 levels. It also increases the Pokémon's friendship a little. One-hit knockout moves are unaffected by X Accuracy. Wonder Launcher [ edit | edit source ] The X Accuracy appears as four Wonder Launcher items, increasing Accuracy by 1, 2, 3, or 6 stages. It costs 3, 5, 7, or 11 points to use respectively.
